title: Equivalence of Zero-One Matrices and Directed Graphs
description: A visual representation showing how a relation can be equivalently represented as both a zero-one matrix and a directed graph, and how computer algorithms translate between these two structures for analysis.
relationships: A relation R from set A to set B defines connections between elements
The Zero-One Matrix is a tabular representation where 1 indicates a connection and 0 indicates none
The Directed Graph represents elements as Nodes and connections as directed Edges
Computer algorithms can generate the Directed Graph from the Matrix and vice versa for analysis tasks like pathfinding or connectivity checking
